Debate.

tweets.text: STEADY LADS
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: I bought $1 million of $STRC today. Will hold it until it reaches par, likely longer.
tweets.full_text: STEADY LADS

tweets.text:
Saylor and Strategy are finally admitting cash is their new priority, as it should be.

And honestly, diluting $MSTR holders for billions of dollars while they still can is the best option they have right now.

The question is how much more the common shareholders are willing to take it and pay the bill before we see even further major compression in the premium, and whether $STRC holders believe it’s enough on a long term basis to believe in the underlying collateral backing their dividends and the $100 peg.

This is why I’ve said there are scenarios where Bitcoin can be bullish at the same time that holding MSTR can be bearish.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Strategy has increased its USD Reserve by $300 million to $1.4 billion and plans to continue replenishing it to support the credit quality of its Digital Credit securities. We also acquired 520 BTC for $35 million, increasing our $BTC Reserve to ₿847,363. $MSTR $STRC

thread_tweets.text:
Saylor and Strategy are finally admitting cash is their new priority, as it should be.

And honestly, diluting $MSTR holders for billions of dollars while they still can is the best option they have right now.

The question is how much more the common shareholders are willing to take it and pay the bill before we see even further major compression in the premium, and whether $STRC holders believe it’s enough on a long term basis to believe in the underlying collateral backing their dividends and the $100 peg.

This is why I’ve said there are scenarios where Bitcoin can be bullish at the same time that holding MSTR can be bearish.
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Strategy has increased its USD Reserve by $300 million to $1.4 billion and plans to continue replenishing it to support the credit quality of its Digital Credit securities. We also acquired 520 BTC for $35 million, increasing our $BTC Reserve to ₿847,363. $MSTR $STRC
thread_tweets.full_text:
Saylor and Strategy are finally admitting cash is their new priority, as it should be.

And honestly, diluting $MSTR holders for billions of dollars while they still can is the best option they have right now.

The question is how much more the common shareholders are willing to take it and pay the bill before we see even further major compression in the premium, and whether $STRC holders believe it’s enough on a long term basis to believe in the underlying collateral backing their dividends and the $100 peg.

This is why I’ve said there are scenarios where Bitcoin can be bullish at the same time that holding MSTR can be bearish.

thread_tweets.text: @_Adrian True, but for more context, you could have also invested $10,000 into a money market fund last July and had somewhere around $10,400 today which would have also outperformed BTC and been close to par with the STRC performance of your chart without any of the residual risk.
thread_tweets.full_text: @_Adrian True, but for more context, you could have also invested $10,000 into a money market fund last July and had somewhere around $10,400 today which would have also outperformed BTC and been close to par with the STRC performance of your chart without any of the residual risk.

thread_tweets.text:
Just saw all of this.

Not sure what benchmark I made in the first place.

Never mentioned anything with a price comparison to BTC for STRC in the first place because it should be incredibly obvious it outperforms while BTC is going lower lol. I just basically said “we’ll see if this is enough on a long turn basis to return STRC to the $100 peg”.

So I felt like a comparison to a MMF was pretty fair as a benchmark comparison for how STRC performs relative to the standard for its product.
thread_tweets.full_text:

thread_tweets.text:
I guess I’m not reading something correctly here.

Thought everything I’ve said was pretty clear and nothing too radical.

Don’t want to get too lost trying to go back and forth in this thread.

Feel like it’d be better if we hold off until Thursday.

Will send something over soon!
thread_tweets.full_text:

tweets.full_text:
Saylor and Strategy are finally admitting cash is their new priority, as it should be.

And honestly, diluting $MSTR holders for billions of dollars while they still can is the best option they have right now.

The question is how much more the common shareholders are willing to take it and pay the bill before we see even further major compression in the premium, and whether $STRC holders believe it’s enough on a long term basis to believe in the underlying collateral backing their dividends and the $100 peg.

This is why I’ve said there are scenarios where Bitcoin can be bullish at the same time that holding MSTR can be bearish.

@_Adrian True, but for more context, you could have also invested $10,000 into a money market fund last July and had somewhere around $10,400 today which would have also outperformed BTC and been close to par with the STRC performance of your chart without any of the residual risk.

Just saw all of this.

Not sure what benchmark I made in the first place.

Never mentioned anything with a price comparison to BTC for STRC in the first place because it should be incredibly obvious it outperforms while BTC is going lower lol. I just basically said “we’ll see if this is enough on a long turn basis to return STRC to the $100 peg”.

So I felt like a comparison to a MMF was pretty fair as a benchmark comparison for how STRC performs relative to the standard for its product.

I guess I’m not reading something correctly here.

Thought everything I’ve said was pretty clear and nothing too radical.

Don’t want to get too lost trying to go back and forth in this thread.

Feel like it’d be better if we hold off until Thursday.

Will send something over soon!

tweets.text: Despite Bitcoin's 2.5% rise, $MSTR is down 2.5%, increasing the discount. That means if MSTR sells more discounted shares to buy Bitcoin, the loss to shareholders will be even greater. Maybe shareholders are finally wising up and selling rather than waiting to be sacrificed.

tweets.text: @saylor For a third week in a row, you sacrificed MSTR common shareholders by selling stock at a discount to buy more Bitcoin. So for a third week in a row, Bitcoin per MSTR share went down. You destroyed value by effectively selling $1.20 worth of Bitcoin to buy $1 worth of Bitcoin.

thread_tweets.text: @SupremeStory1 @saylor None, just a fraction of a Bitcoin in my Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. So far the Bitcoin in that reserve are worth less than when they were gifted to the reserve.

thread_tweets.text: @saint_hodl @saylor No, the shares he sold represented $1.2 worth of Bitcoin. Then be bought just $1 worth of Bitcoin with the proceeds. So MSTR shares are now backed by fewer Bitcoin then they were prior to the sale. How can you not understand that?
thread_tweets.full_text: @saint_hodl @saylor No, the shares he sold represented $1.2 worth of Bitcoin. Then be bought just $1 worth of Bitcoin with the proceeds. So MSTR shares are now backed by fewer Bitcoin then they were prior to the sale. How can you not understand that?

thread_tweets.text: @saylor Also, if you wanted to increase your dollar reserves the prudent thing to do when MSTR shares trade at a large discount is to sell Bitcoin. That way Bitcoin per share would go up, not down. That would add to shareholder value, but it would also crash Bitcoin. So you can't do it.
thread_tweets.full_text: @saylor Also, if you wanted to increase your dollar reserves the prudent thing to do when MSTR shares trade at a large discount is to sell Bitcoin. That way Bitcoin per share would go up, not down. That would add to shareholder value, but it would also crash Bitcoin. So you can't do it.

thread_tweets.text: @Harsch_Reality @saylor Effectively, selling BTC would be better than selling shares, because BTC per share would be higher under that scenario. When MSTR sells shares at a 20% discount to BTC NAV, it receives only $0.80 of value for every $1.00 of Bitcoin exposure it gives away.
